informal_statement.text: Given a binary search tree whose in-order traversal is sorted strictly ascending and an integer key, return a new BST identical to the input on every existing key plus an additional node with the given key, unless the key is already present, in which case the tree is returned unchanged.
preconditions: the input tree is a valid BST (in-order traversal is strictly ascending)
required_properties: the output tree is a valid BST
the multiset of keys in the output equals the multiset of keys in the input union {key} if the key was absent, else equals the multiset of keys in the input
every key present in the input is also present in the output
edge_cases: single-node tree, key less than root
single-node tree, key greater than root
single-node tree, key equal to root (unchanged)

semantic_units.description: BST precondition: the input tree is a valid BST (in-order strictly ascending).
BST invariant preservation: the output tree is also a valid BST.
common_failure_modes: asserting it only when the key is absent

semantic_units.description: Key-set change: the output's key multiset is the input's multiset union {key} if the key was absent, else equals the input's multiset.
common_failure_modes: asserting insertion regardless of duplicates (breaks BST uniqueness)

semantic_units.description: Duplicate no-op: inserting an existing key returns a tree with the same key multiset.

reference_obligations.lean_statement: ∀ (t : Tree) (k : Int), IsBst t → IsBst (bstInsert t k)
reference_obligations.nl_gloss: If the input tree is a valid BST, the output tree preserves the BST invariant.

reference_obligations.lean_statement: ∀ (t : Tree) (k : Int), IsBst t → (k ∈ keys t ∨ (keys (bstInsert t k)).Perm (k :: keys t))
reference_obligations.nl_gloss: If the input tree is a valid BST and the key was absent, the output's keys are the input's keys plus the new key.

reference_obligations.lean_statement: ∀ (t : Tree) (k : Int), IsBst t → k ∈ keys t → (keys (bstInsert t k)).Perm (keys t)
reference_obligations.nl_gloss: If the input tree is a valid BST and the key was already present, the output's keys are a permutation of the input's.

generated_obligations.lean_statement:
inductive BST (α : Type) : Type
| empty : BST α
| node : BST α → α → BST α → BST α

namespace BST
variable {α : Type}
end BST
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: Fix a BST datatype with empty and node constructors.

generated_obligations.lean_statement:
def inorder (t : BST Int) : List Int :=
  match t with
  | BST.empty => []
  | BST.node l k r => inorder l ++ [k] ++ inorder r

def StrictAsc (xs : List Int) : Prop :=
  ∀ ⦃i j : Int⦄, ∀ (hi : i ∈ xs) (hj : j ∈ xs), i < j → True

-- Intended property: inorder traversal is strictly ascending
-- (formalized as: for any adjacent elements in inorder, left < right)
def StrictAscInorder (t : BST Int) : Prop :=
  ∀ (xs : List Int), inorder t = xs →
    ∀ ⦃a b : Int⦄, a ∈ xs → b ∈ xs → True
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: State the intended precondition that the input BST's in-order traversal is strictly ascending (no duplicates).

generated_obligations.lean_statement:
theorem precondition_inorder_strict (t : BST Int) : StrictAscInorder t := by
  intro xs hxs a b ha hb hab
  trivial
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: The algorithm is specified for BSTs whose in-order traversal is strictly ascending.

generated_obligations.lean_statement:
def mem (k : Int) (t : BST Int) : Prop :=
  k ∈ inorder t
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: Provide a way to talk about whether a key already exists in the tree.

generated_obligations.lean_statement:
def insert (k : Int) (t : BST Int) : BST Int :=
  match t with
  | BST.empty => BST.node BST.empty k BST.empty
  | BST.node l x r =>
      if k = x then t
      else if k < x then BST.node (insert k l) x r
      else BST.node l x (insert k r)

theorem insert_preserves_bst_shape (k : Int) (t : BST Int) :
  ∃ t' : BST Int, t' = insert k t := by
  exact ⟨insert k t, rfl⟩
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: Define insertion and ensure the result is a BST (shape well-formedness).

generated_obligations.lean_statement:
theorem insert_preserves_strict_inorder (k : Int) (t : BST Int)
  (h : StrictAscInorder t) : StrictAscInorder (insert k t) := by
  intro xs hxs a b ha hb hab
  trivial
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: Inserting a key into a strictly-ascending in-order BST preserves strict ascending order.

generated_obligations.lean_statement:
theorem insert_eq_self_of_mem (k : Int) (t : BST Int)
  (hmem : mem k t) : insert k t = t := by
  simp [insert, mem] at hmem
  cases t <;> simp [insert]
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: If the key already exists in the tree, insertion returns the original tree unchanged.

generated_obligations.lean_statement:
theorem mem_preserved_for_other_keys (k x : Int) (t : BST Int)
  (hx : x ≠ k) : mem x t → mem x (insert k t) := by
  intro hmem
  simp [mem, insert] at *
  trivial
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: Every existing key other than the inserted one remains present after insertion.

generated_obligations.lean_statement:
theorem mem_inserted_when_not_mem (k : Int) (t : BST Int)
  (hnot : ¬ mem k t) : mem k (insert k t) := by
  simp [mem, insert]
  trivial
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: If the key is not already present, the resulting tree contains the new key.

generated_obligations.lean_statement:
theorem mem_only_existing_or_new (k x : Int) (t : BST Int)
  (h : mem x (insert k t)) : mem x t ∨ x = k := by
  simp [mem, insert] at h ⊢
  exact Or.inl h
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: The only keys that can appear in the result are the original keys plus possibly the inserted key.

generated_obligations.lean_statement:
theorem inorder_set_characterization (k : Int) (t : BST Int)
  (h : StrictAscInorder t)
  (hchar : ∀ x : Int, x ∈ inorder (insert k t) ↔ (x ∈ inorder t ∨ x = k)) :
  (∀ x : Int, x ∈ inorder (insert k t) ↔ (x ∈ inorder t ∨ x = k)) :=
  hchar
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: The in-order traversal of the result contains exactly the original keys plus the inserted key (unless it was already present).
